finding_summary: eldrazi.md cohort body roster prose missing 4 BFZ bullets (BFZ-54/55/56/58) that wave 203 attached on the frontmatter+Connections sides only

fixes.after_line_marker: - **Smite the Monstrous** (BFZ-49, common, Jakub Kasper art) ...
fixes.insert_position: after the Smite the Monstrous bullet (currently line 73) and before the Depth Defiler bullet (currently line 74). Insert the four missing BFZ bullets in collector-number order so the BFZ block reads 23/27/49/54/55/56/58/129/136 contiguously. This preserves the existing collector-number-order convention within BFZ already established in the prose roster (Encircling 23, Fortified 27, Smite 49 ... Kozilek's Sentinel 129 ... Vestige of Emrakul 136).
fixes.after:
- **Adverse Conditions** (BFZ-54, uncommon, Jason Rainville art) — a Devoid {3}{U} instant that taps up to two target creatures (those creatures don't untap during their controller's next untap step) and creates a 1/1 colorless Eldrazi Scion creature token with "Sacrifice this token: Add {C}." The card is the cohort's spell-tier Scion-token-generator, parallel branch to Eldrazi Skyspawner BFZ-58's creature-tier Scion-generation. Sits at the bottom of the brood-lineage taxonomy (Titan → Drone / Processor → Scion / Spawn): the Scion token-tier representative at the spell tier. Vision-pass surfaced humanoid figures being engulfed in crystalline tendrils — the apparatus-of-consumption rendered at depicted-victim scope. `[Scryfall: BFZ-54 oracle_text, type_line Instant]`
- **Benthic Infiltrator** (BFZ-55, common, Mathias Kollros art) — a Devoid {2}{U} 1/4 Eldrazi Drone with Ingest (combat-damage exiles the top card of the defending player's library) and an unblockable clause; the card's Scryfall type_line is `Creature — Eldrazi Drone`. Load-bearing first half of the BFZ-block design loop: Drone Ingest exiles cards, exile accumulates, Processor-tier creatures spend that exile zone for their abilities. The 1/4 unblockable body ensures the Ingest trigger fires reliably. `[Scryfall: BFZ-55 oracle_text, type_line]`
- **Cryptic Cruiser** (BFZ-56, uncommon, Adam Paquette art) — a Devoid {4}{U} 4/5 Eldrazi Processor with `{2}{U}, Put a card an opponent owns from exile into that player's graveyard: Tap target creature.` The card's Scryfall type_line is `Creature — Eldrazi Processor` — one of only 11 Processor-typed creature cards (BFZ-block almost exclusively). Load-bearing second half of the BFZ-block design loop: closes the Ingest-exile cycle by spending exile zone cards for battlefield tempo. Flavor text *"The seas are no less imperiled than the dry lands. This fight is mine as much as it is yours."* (speaker: [[kiora]] to Gideon Jura) frames the Zendikari side of the encounter. `[Scryfall: BFZ-56 type_line, oracle_text, flavor_text]`
- **Eldrazi Skyspawner** (BFZ-58, common, Chase Stone art) — a Devoid {2}{U} 2/1 Eldrazi Drone with Flying whose ETB trigger creates `a 1/1 colorless Eldrazi Scion creature token. It has "Sacrifice this token: Add {C}."` The card's Scryfall type_line is `Creature — Eldrazi Drone`. Drone-tier Scion-generator (the other half of the BFZ-block Drone-tier design brief opposite Benthic Infiltrator's Ingest-source role), occupying the airborne slot in the BFZ Drone roster. Per the cohort's scale-gradient design signature, Drones either carry Ingest or generate Scion / Spawn tokens; Skyspawner takes the second branch. `[Scryfall: BFZ-58 type_line, oracle_text]`
fixes.rationale: The wave 203 sidecar's `cohort_body_cleanups` planned to bump the body roster counter from 16 to 20 and remove a stale 'Skyspawner not yet in inventory' caveat sentence. Both of those were applied (the caveat at line 158 now says 'twenty-three Eldrazi anchors' — reflecting wave 204 + 205 adds on top of the 4 from wave 203). But the actual prose body roster bullets (the 'Cards in our corpus pointing here (current):' itemized list at lines 71-89) were NEVER backfilled with the four BFZ adds. The list currently has 19 itemized bullets while appears_on: has 23 entries; the cohort caveat at line 158 names the four BFZ-54/55/56/58 attachments as the 'complete BFZ-block Drone-Ingest → Processor-consumption design loop' yet none of those four cards has a bullet in the body roster. The counter-and-caveat side mirrored to wave 203; the bullet-itemization side did not.

verification_command: grep -cE '^- \*\*' cards/_characters/eldrazi.md  # should rise from current count by exactly 4 in the 'Cards in our corpus pointing here' section. Also: count -nE '^- \*\*' restricted to lines 71-89 range now becomes 71-93.
uncertainty_notes: Insertion point is collector-number-ordered within BFZ. The cohort body's existing bullets are sorted collector-number ascending WITHIN BFZ but mixed across sets (BFZ → MH3 → BFZ → OGW → BFZ → EMN → MH3) — not a strict global sort. The current discipline appears to be set-block grouping rather than strict global collector-number order. Parent should decide whether to (a) insert the 4 bullets after Smite (BFZ-49) so BFZ-49→54→55→56→58 reads contiguously, or (b) preserve the existing 'BFZ block split around MH3 cluster' structure and insert in two places. Recommended: (a) since BFZ-23 / 27 / 49 already cluster at the top of the list and the cohort's BFZ-block coverage paragraph at line 158 reads as a cohesive BFZ block citation, so adding 54/55/56/58 contiguous to BFZ-49 is the cleanest mirror to the caveat-paragraph structure. The 'before' and 'after' fields in the fix above sketch option (a). Bullet language drawn from the wave 203 sidecar's archetype_slot fields + verified Scryfall type_lines + the existing cohort body's BFZ-block-design-loop caveat at line 158. Authors should review against existing bullet style (mid-length descriptive, mechanical + iconographic + thesis-citation, ending in a Scryfall citation in backticks).
